treatment_information.specific_treatment: esophagectomy, esophagogastrostomy through the esophageal bed and 2-field lymphadenectomy
treatment_information.treatment_response: No significant complications occurred after surgery

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: paclitaxel (200mg/m2 on day 1 for 3hours) plus cisplatin (30mg/m2 per day for 3 days) repeated for 3 weeks
treatment_information.treatment_response: No significant complications occurred after administration of adjuvant radio-chemotherapy

treatment_information.specific_treatment: radiotherapy against large chest irregular area, including the entire esophageal bed and the mediastina
treatment_information.dosage_or_details: The total dose for 95% of the planning target volume (PTV) was 50.4Gy/28 fractions, and for 95% of the PTV for involved lymph nodes (PTVnd) was 56Gy/28 fractions
treatment_information.treatment_response: No significant complications occurred after administration of adjuvant radio-chemotherapy

treatment_timeline.event: Patient presented with difficulty in swallowing
Biopsy of the lesion under endoscopy demonstrated a mucoepidermoid carcinoma of the esophagus
Esophagectomy, esophagogastrostomy through the esophageal bed and 2-field lymphadenectomy performed
Histopathological analysis of the tumor revealed histological characteristics typical of an esophageal MEC
Radio-chemotherapy was administered to the patient
Esophageal CT scan revealed that the wall of esophagus was evenly thickened. Endoscopic assessment revealed no evidence of recurrence
Esophageal CT scan showed a thickened esophageal wall
Esophageal CT scan showed a thickened esophageal wall. Endoscopic assessment revealed no esophageal stricture and no evidence of recurrence
Patient is alive with no dysphagia and no evidence of recurrence

patient_outcome.final_status: Alive with no dysphagia and no evidence of recurrence for over 39 months
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Follow-up at 17, 19, and 31 months after surgery with CT scans and endoscopic assessments
